title: Who Is IN For The 2026 IBJJF Brasileiros? Notable Entries At Black Belt
author: Joe The IBJJF
content: 2026 BRASILEIRO JIU-JITSU IBJJF Who Is IN For The 2026 IBJJF Brasileiros? Notable Entries At Black Belt Check out all the biggest black belt names entered for the IBJJF Brazilian Nationals, going down on FloGrappling April 24th-May 3rd. Apr 8, 2026 by Joe Gilpin The IBJJF Brazilian National Championship is right around the corner and registration is now closed, with participants having reached almost 8,000 (a new record for the event). With the divisions roughly set (tho some may move to a new weight class), it's time to see who is in for one of the year's most prestigious events. The IBJJF Brazilian National Championship is the third of their four annual major championships, joining the European, Pan, and World Championships. Both Euros and Pans delivered some great action, with thrilling finals rounds and world-class matches every round leading up. With Brasileiros already reaching capacity, I think that's a sign there is a lot of excitement and energy that will translate into an event to remember. The tournament kicks off April 24th and runs through May 3rd, with black belts taking the mats the final two days. There are a ton of great divisions to watch and nine world #1''s in the mix. The biggest story coming in will have to be the women's absolute where Sarah Galvão and Gabi Pessanha have developed an incredible rivalry. Separated by almost 100lbs, Sarah and Gabi have split absolute finals 1-1 in the first two IBJJF majors this year. Whoever wins here carries the momentum into Worlds - where even more is on the line. For those on Grand Slam watch, only three black belts are on track. They are: Diego Pato, light-feather Tainan Dalpra, middle Gabi Pessanha, super-heavy Because the IBJJF considers the competition calendar to begin at Worlds and end at Brasileiros, Diego, Tainan, and Gabi can all win the organization's official Grand Slam by taking gold in São Paulo. Some of the most notable names per division (ranking according to FloGrappling's official men's and women's world rankings); Men's Rooster #2 Jalen Fonacier #5 Yuri Hendrex #6 Andrew Soares #10 Oziel
